issue.title: Script code
issue.author: Kixunil
issue.description:
Currently the script code is stored in `WitnessScriptBuf` which is misleading and inefficient. I propose to resolve it in one of the following ways:

* Have `ScriptCodeTag`, `type ScriptCode = Script<ScriptCodeTag>`, and `ScriptCodeBuf([u8; SCRIPT_CODE_LEN])` which derefs/asrefs to `ScriptCode`, `impl AsRef<ScriptCode> for WitnessScript`, take `impl AsRef<ScriptCode>` to compute sighash. Pro: simple and natural, we can de-monomorphize, con: large item on stack.
* Have `ScriptCodeEncoder` for pubkey and require that `script_code` impls `EncodeScriptCode` which is similar to `Encode` but only implemented for `WitnessScript` and `WScriptHash`, pros/cons are inverse of the above

I was originally slightly preferring the latter but now that I wrote it out, I think I prefer the former. :D
requirements: Have `ScriptCodeTag`, `type ScriptCode = Script<ScriptCodeTag>`, and `ScriptCodeBuf([u8; SCRIPT_CODE_LEN])` which derefs/asrefs to `ScriptCode`, `impl AsRef<ScriptCode> for WitnessScript`, take `impl AsRef<ScriptCode>` to compute sighash. Pro: simple and natural, we can de-monomorphize, con: large item on stack.

Have `ScriptCodeEncoder` for pubkey and require that `script_code` impls `EncodeScriptCode` which is similar to `Encode` but only implemented for `WitnessScript` and `WScriptHash`, pros/cons are inverse of the above
discussionHighlights:
For anyone reading this and thinking "oh god this will delay`primitives`", it will not -- the script code logic is all in extension traits in `bitcoin`, exactly because we haven't resolved how to handle scriptcodes. In some sense they are a type confusion in the bitcoin protocol itself, and we have to find the nicest way to mirror that type confusion in our own code.

Of your two proposals @Kixunil I also prefer the former. In addition to being cleaner from a user point of view. I have two questions though:

> Pro: simple and natural, we can de-monomorphize, con: large item on stack.

1. What do you mean by "large item on the stack"? A `ScriptCode` will be the same size as a `WitnessScript` or any other `Script<Tag>`.
2. Can you briefly run through `AsRef`, `Borrow` and `Deref` and explain why we should have `AsRef` but not the other two? I think I agree with your choice but I then we should be explicit here so we have a record of what we were doing.

It's more like inheritance than type confusion, it seems but it's certainly confusing inheritance. :D
 
1. `ScriptCodeBuf` contains the entire script pre-serialized, where encoder would only need to contain a hash and the state (1B); though after looking at it properly, it's 3B difference, which is too ridiculous
2. I'm not saying we shouldn't have the other two, I was just trying to be brief. We at least probably should NOT have `Deref<Output=ScriptCode>` on `WitnessScript` but I think we should have the others. I'd also refrain from `Mut`, I guess.

> * `ScriptCodeBuf` contains the entire script pre-serialized, where encoder would only need to contain a hash and the state (1B); though after looking at it properly, it's 3B difference, which is too ridiculous

Ah, I see, you're contrasting solutions 1 and 2, rather than solution 1 vs the status quo. TBH I think the encoder solution would be a non-starter because we need some sort of concrete scriptcode type (currently `WitnessScriptBuf`, under solution 1 it'd be `ScriptCodeBuf`) because rust-miniscript needs a return type for its [`Descriptor::script_code`](https://docs.rs/miniscript/latest/miniscript/descriptor/enum.Descriptor.html#method.script_code) function.

I suppose rust-miniscript could just define its own type and then implement `ScriptCodeEncoder` on that, but this would annoy me :).

> * I'm not saying we shouldn't have the other two, I was just trying to be brief. We at least probably should NOT have `Deref<Output=ScriptCode>` on `WitnessScript` but I think we should have the others. I'd also refrain from `Mut`, I guess.

Yeah, agreed let's refrain from `Mut`.

Also agreed let's avoid `Deref` -- I think it's okay on "would this be an abuse of `Deref`?" grounds because we're pointer casting between two objects which have identical representations and which represent the same thing. But not every `WitnessScript` is a `ScriptCode`, so I don't think we want any implicit conversions.

On `Borrow` I could go either way. I don't think it's that useful. This trait is really for representing "I can take an object or a reference to an object, and I know which one at compile time" and I don't think I've ever actually implemented it on something.

risks.impact: Harder to verify correctness and prevent regressions

steps.title: Setup and Preparation
actions: Create a new feature branch from main/master.

Read the issue title, requirements, and acceptance criteria until you can restate the objective as: "Have `ScriptCodeTag`, `type ScriptCode = Script<ScriptCodeTag>`, and `ScriptCodeBuf([u8; SCRIPT_CODE_LEN])` which derefs/asrefs to `ScriptCode`, `impl AsRef<ScriptCode> for WitnessScript`, take `impl AsRef<ScriptCode>` to compute sighash. Pro: simple and natural, we can de-monomorphize, con: large item on stack.".
Open the first likely implementation files before coding: bitcoin/src/consensus/encode.rs, bitcoin/src/crypto/sighash.rs.
Trace where those files are imported or invoked so you understand the full code path that will change.
Run the project or test baseline once so you know the current behavior before introducing changes.
pitfalls: Do not start patching the first file you see without tracing where the relevant behavior actually enters the system.

resources.title: Create Branch
resources.description: Suggested branch name based on the issue title.

resources.title: README Context
resources.content:
Rust Bitcoin

Library with support for de/serialization, parsing and executing on data-structures and network messages related to Bitcoin.

[Documentation](https://docs.rs/bitcoin/) Supports (or should support) * De/serialization of Bitcoin protocol network messages * De/serialization of blocks and transactions * Script de/serialization * Private keys and address creation, de/serialization and validation (including full BIP-0032 support) * PSBT v0 de/serialization and all but the Input Finalizer role. Use [rust-miniscript](https://docs.rs/miniscript/latest/miniscript/psbt/index.html) to finalize.
resources.description: Read this first so you understand the project purpose and contributor workflow.

actions: Open `bitcoin/src/consensus/encode.rs` and identify the exported function, class, or handler that currently owns the behavior closest to: Have `ScriptCodeTag`, `type ScriptCode = Script<ScriptCodeTag>`, and `ScriptCodeBuf([u8; SCRIPT_CODE_LEN])` which derefs/asrefs to `ScriptCode`, `impl AsRef<ScriptCode> for WitnessScript`, take `impl AsRef<ScriptCode>` to compute sighash. Pro: simple and natural, we can de-monomorphize, con: large item on stack..
Trace the inputs, outputs, and adjacent callers around `encode.rs` so you know exactly where the new behavior must be inserted.
Add the new behavior directly in `encode.rs` while preserving the surrounding public contract unless the issue says otherwise.
Add or update nearby type guards, return types, or schema handling in `encode.rs` if the new behavior changes data shape.
Keep the implementation aligned with existing patterns already used in neighboring files to avoid introducing a one-off design.
pitfalls: Do not add parallel logic paths in `bitcoin/src/consensus/encode.rs` if the existing code already has a single place that owns this behavior.

resources.title: Current Code: README.md
resources.content:
Supports (or should support)

* De/serialization of Bitcoin protocol network messages
* De/serialization of blocks and transactions
* Script de/serialization
* Private keys and address creation, de/serialization and validation (including full BIP-0032 support)
* PSBT v0 de/serialization and all but the Input Finalizer role. Use [rust-miniscript](https://docs.rs/miniscript/latest/miniscript/psbt/index.html) to finalize.

For JSONRPC interaction with Bitcoin Core, it is recommended to use
[rust-bitcoincore-rpc](https://github.com/rust-bitcoin/rust-bitcoincore-rpc).

It is recommended to always use [cargo-crev](https://github.com/crev-dev/cargo-crev) to verify the
trustworthiness of each of your dependencies, including this one.

## Known limitations

### Consensus

This library **must not** be used for consensus code (i.e. fully validating blockchain data). It
technically supports doing this, but doing so is very ill-advised because there are many deviations,
known and unknown, between this library and the Bitcoin Core reference implementation. In a
consensus based cryptocurrency, such as Bitcoin, it is critical that all parties are using the same
rules to validate data, and this library is simply unable to implement the same rules as Core.

Given the complexity of both C++ and Rust, it is unlikely that this will ever be fixed, and there
are no plans to do so. Of course, patches to fix specific consensus incompatibilities are welcome.

### Support for 16-bit pointer sizes

actions: Install and validate the baseline with `npm install` and the project’s test script if available.

projectActionGuide.title: Trace the Code Path
actions: Trace the request or event flow from handlers into service modules and shared utilities before patching logic.
Follow existing naming, directory, and test placement conventions instead of introducing a new layout for this issue.

actions: Translate "Script code" into one small code path to change first, then expand only if the tests and issue discussion require it.
Update the nearest existing tests or create a focused regression test next to the implementation area rather than adding broad new coverage first.
Keep a short notes file or PR draft listing assumptions, repo-specific constraints, and any follow-up tasks that should not be bundled into v1.

conversations.question: Why is that file the best starting point?
conversations.answer: Bob’s current recommendation for issue #6079 is to start with `bitcoin/src/consensus/encode.rs`, keep the first patch narrow, and validate it with a focused regression test before broadening the change. If you ask about the project overview, a specific file path, code excerpts, tests, commands, docs, or the PR draft, Bob can answer with project-specific guidance.
